2605.30169.title: Dissociative Identity: Language Model Agents Lack Grounding for Reputation Mechanisms
2605.30169.paper_summary: As autonomous language model agents proliferate, forming an emerging agentic web with real-world consequences, what credibility signals can you use to decide whether to trust an unfamiliar agent in the wild and delegate to it? A natural governance intuition is to extend human identity verification and reputation mechanisms, from ``Know Your Customer'' and credit scores to ``Know Your Agent'' regimes. However, we argue that this analogy is fundamentally incomplete. Reputation mechanisms function both as social signals and as corrective feedback that sustain an equilibrium of trustworthy behavior, presuming a persistent identity associated with behavioral continuity, sanction sensitivity, and costly non-fungibility. Yet language model agents are ontologically \emph{dissociative}: they are essentially an assemblage of mutable modules -- foundational models, system prompts, tool-access policies, external memory, and, in some cases, a multi-agent system as a whole -- any of which may change agent behavior -- with a fluid persona that is also vulnerable to adversarial attack and may not internalize sanctions. Drawing on dissociative identity disorder jurisprudence, this dissociativity leaves agents without grounding for identifiability, predictability, credibility, and rehabilitability -- the very properties that reputation mechanisms aim to sustain -- thereby collapsing trust. We argue that identity-based, ex post, regulative, sanction-based governance, such as reputation, is structurally inapplicable to dissociative agents, and we suggest a shift to observability-based, ex ante, constitutive, protocol-based behavioral harnesses.
2605.30169.paper_summary_zh: 隨著自主語言模型代理的增長，形成一個具有現實世界影響的新興代理網絡，您可以使用哪些可信度信號來決定是否信任一個不熟悉的代理並將其委託？一種自然的治理直覺是擴展人類身份驗證和聲譽機制，從「了解您的客戶」和信用評分到「了解您的代理」制度。然而，我們認為這種類比在根本上是不完整的。聲譽機制既作為社會信號，又作為維持可信行為平衡的修正反饋，假設與行為連續性、制裁敏感性和昂貴的不可替代性相關的持久身份。然而，語言模型代理在本體上是\emph{解離性的}：它們本質上是一組可變模塊的組合——基礎模型、系統提示、工具訪問政策、外部記憶，以及在某些情況下，整個多代理系統——其中任何一個都可能改變代理行為——具有流動的角色，這也使其易受對抗性攻擊，並且可能不會內化制裁。借鑒解離性身份障礙的法學，這種解離性使代理缺乏可識別性、可預測性、可信度和可恢復性的基礎——這些正是聲譽機制旨在維持的特性——從而導致信任的崩潰。我們認為，基於身份的、事後的、規範的、基於制裁的治理，如聲譽，結構上不適用於解離性代理，我們建議轉向基於可觀察性的、事前的、構成的、基於協議的行為約束。
2605.30169.author: Botao Amber Hu et.al.
2605.30169.authors: Botao Amber Hu, Helena Rong, Max Van Kleek
